sections.title: Market Pulse: Inflation Concerns Weigh on Wall Street

items.headline: Major Indices Dip as Hotter-than-Expected CPI Fuels Inflation Fears
items.summary: U.S. equities are showing a downturn today, May 12, with the S&P 500, Nasdaq, and Dow Jones Industrial Average all trading lower. A hotter-than-expected Consumer Price Index (CPI) report for April, coupled with persistent geopolitical tensions surrounding the Iran war and rising oil prices, is dampening market sentiment. While some AI-related stocks continued to show resilience, broader market performance reflects concerns over inflation and potential shifts in monetary policy.

items.statContext: Fell 270 points, with technology stocks experiencing pullbacks.

sections.title: Top Movers: Notable Gainers and Losers

items.headline: Zebra Technologies Soars on Earnings Beat
items.summary: Zebra Technologies ($ZBRA) surged 15.4% in pre-market trading after the asset-tracking technology company reported stronger-than-expected quarterly earnings.

items.headline: Amentum Holdings Rises on Strong Earnings
items.summary: Amentum Holdings ($AMTM) gained 3.4% in pre-market trading after surpassing quarterly earnings estimates.

items.headline: NVIDIA Continues Upward Momentum on AI Demand
items.summary: NVIDIA ($NVDA) rose 2% (May 11 data), with analysts reiterating 'overweight' ratings and raising price targets ahead of its May 20 earnings, driven by sustained AI infrastructure demand.

items.headline: Intel Dips Amid Profit-Taking
items.summary: Intel ($INTC) was down 4.3% in pre-market trading, largely attributed to profit-taking following previous gains.

items.headline: DaVita Slips on Profit-Taking
items.summary: Shares of DaVita ($DVA) slipped 4% in pre-market, with analysts citing profit-taking after a significant rally.

items.headline: Palantir Down in Premarket Trading
items.summary: Palantir Technologies ($PLTR) shares were down 1.3% in pre-market, despite news of its collaboration with Ukraine on AI-based drone defense.

sections.title: Market News: Economic Data, Geopolitics, and Earnings

items.headline: April CPI Exceeds Expectations, Fueling Inflation Worries
items.summary: The Consumer Price Index (CPI) for April came in hotter than anticipated, rising 3.8% annually, with core CPI increasing 0.4% monthly and 2.8% annually. This indicates inflation remains stubbornly above the Federal Reserve's 2% target, prompting investor concern.

items.headline: Kevin Warsh Poised to Become New Fed Chair This Week
items.summary: Kevin Warsh's nomination for Federal Reserve Chair cleared a key Senate hurdle and he is expected to succeed Jerome Powell by Friday, May 16. Warsh is viewed as more hawkish and inflation-focused, which could influence future monetary policy.

items.headline: Iran War Escalation Drives Oil Prices Higher, Stoking Inflation
items.summary: Uncertainty surrounding the Iran war continues to push oil prices upwards, with Brent crude trading above $101-$107 per barrel. President Trump's rejection of Iran's peace proposal has raised fears of a prolonged conflict and disruptions to global oil supply, contributing significantly to inflationary pressures.

items.headline: Q1 Earnings Season Nears End with Strong Tech Performance
items.summary: The Q1 2026 earnings season is in its final 'peak week,' with the S&P 500 projected to deliver a robust 27.7% earnings growth, largely propelled by a 50% expansion in the Information Technology sector. Approximately 84% of reporting S&P 500 companies have beaten EPS estimates.

items.headline: Energy and Materials Lead, Technology and Communication Services Lag
items.summary: Today's market saw the Energy Select Sector SPDR ($XLE) advance 2.6%, and Materials Select Sector SPDR ($XLB) rise 1.4%, benefiting from higher commodity prices. Conversely, the Communication Services Select Sector SPDR ($XLC) declined 2.3%, and the Technology Select Sector SPDR ($XLK) fell 1.11%, indicating some profit-taking and rotation out of growth sectors.
